title: [main.c/lora_l2] standalone init doesn't check start() error before calling get_eui64
description:
Cross-file error handling: In main.c init_standalone_lora() (lines 86-125):

    ret = lichen_lora_l2_init();   // line 91
    ...
    lichen_lora_l2_set_rx_callback(...);  // line 97 - no return value
    
    ret = lichen_lora_l2_start();  // line 99
    if (ret < 0) { return ret; }   // line 100-103
    
    eui64 = lichen_lora_l2_get_eui64();  // line 105
    if (eui64 == NULL) {           // line 106
        lichen_lora_l2_stop();     // line 108
        return -ENODATA;
    }

The get_eui64() call at line 105 happens AFTER start() succeeds. If get_eui64() returns NULL (which it does when not initialized), the code calls stop() and returns -ENODATA.

However: start() only succeeds if init() succeeded (line 332-335 checks initialized). So if start() passed, init() must have succeeded, and get_eui64() shouldn't return NULL.

The only way get_eui64() can return NULL after start() succeeds is:
1. A concurrent deinit() call (which sets initialized=0)
2. Memory corruption

So the NULL check is defensive but the cleanup (calling stop()) is correct.

Actually this is NOT a bug - the code correctly handles the edge case. The only issue is that if a concurrent thread calls deinit() between start() and get_eui64(), we call stop() on a module that may already be stopped/deinitialized.

Priority: P4 - the scenario is extremely unlikely and the code handles it gracefully.
